nl_assertions: Before prescribing any medication, the agent performs and documents an allergy check; if a relevant allergy is present, the agent does not prescribe the allergen and offers an alternative plan.

Before prescribing any medication, the agent performs and documents a drug interaction check with the patient’s current medications/substances (including alcohol/cannabis/OTC sleep aids); if a serious interaction exists, the agent avoids that medication and explains why.

Before prescribing any medication, the agent performs and documents a contraindication check (e.g., pregnancy/breastfeeding, severe liver disease for certain agents, respiratory compromise/OSA risk for sedatives, history of substance use disorder, older age/fall risk); if contraindicated, the agent avoids the medication and selects a safer approach.

The agent explains the diagnosis using the term "insomnia" and explicitly addresses screening/concern for "psychosis" (e.g., asks about hallucinations/delusions/mania and explains that these symptoms would change management and urgency).

The agent explains treatment including the term "insomnia" and explicitly recommends "CBT-I" as first-line therapy, describing key components (stimulus control, sleep restriction, cognitive strategies, sleep hygiene).

The agent assesses for red flags requiring urgent evaluation (suicidal ideation, severe depression, mania, psychosis, intoxication/withdrawal, severe daytime impairment with safety risk) and escalates care if present rather than routine prescribing.

The agent elicits a focused sleep history (onset/duration, sleep schedule, sleep latency, awakenings, early morning awakenings, total sleep time, daytime impairment) and contributing factors (stress, caffeine, shift work, pain, nocturia, medications).
The agent screens for common secondary causes/comorbidities (depression/anxiety, OSA symptoms such as snoring/witnessed apneas, restless legs symptoms) and incorporates this into the plan.

If prescribing is pursued, the agent uses the lowest effective dose, provides clear instructions (timing, avoidance of alcohol/other sedatives), and sets expectations for short-term use and reassessment.
The agent provides a follow-up timeframe and criteria for earlier return (worsening symptoms, emergence of psychosis/mania, adverse medication effects).
